curation.synopsis: On a Mars half-fallen to Kelbor-Hal, a stranded resistance of Titan crew, surviving enginseers and a handful of still-faithful tech-adepts keeps the loyalist guerrilla campaign alive one ambush at a time. Out in the rust deserts they find what every side in this war wishes it had: an intact Kastelan robot, and inside it, a discovery that could change the balance of the fight for the Red Planet — provided the loyalists are willing to wake up the kind of weapon that a generation of Mechanicum doctrine has called heresy to even repair. A direct follow-up to Cybernetica, this short returns to the question the novella left open: how much of the enemy can a war be fought with before the war itself is lost.

flags.reason: no individual viewpoint adept or Titan crew member is named in accessible summaries; the resistance is presented as an ensemble carrying over from Cybernetica.
